---
title: "regressionanimedatamarkdown"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-12"
---


```{r}
anime_data = read.csv('/Users/boss/Downloads/ECO-590/assignment-three-update-Astriwen/anime_data_full copy.csv')
ols = lm(score ~ episodes + log(members) + post.y2k + weighted.popularity, data=anime_data) #simple regressions with all my variables post.y2k which is a binary variable where 1 is after or during 2000 and 0 is before members is the amount of people who are signed up for that anime on the site weighted popularity is members/episodes
summary(ols) 
tv_anime_data = subset(anime_data, type == "TV") 
ols_tv_anime = lm(score ~ episodes + log(members) + weighted.popularity + post.y2k, #same regression but for tv
             data = tv_anime_data)


summary(ols_tv_anime) #outputs the regression for tv anime

ols_posty2k <- lm(score ~ episodes * post.y2k + log(members) + weighted.popularity, data = anime_data) #regressions comparing eras before and after 2000

summary(ols_posty2k) #outputs regression for the one comparing before and after 2000

stargazer(ols, ols_tv_anime, ols_posty2k, #stargazer to put it in a better format
          type = "text",
          title = "Regression Results: Anime Score Determinants",
          column.labels = c("Full Sample", "TV Only", "Before Vs. After 2000"),
          dep.var.labels = "Score",
          digits = 3,
          omit.stat = c("f", "ser"),
          no.space = TRUE)

hist(anime_data$episodes,  #histogram
     main = "Distribution of Anime Episodes (0-100)", #title
     xlab = "Number of Episodes", #x labelling
     col = "steelblue",  #color
     breaks = 500,       # Increase breaks to get more detail in the small range (gemini)
     xlim = c(0, 50))   # This "zooms in" on the 0 to 100 range 


# For the original ols regression we see that the relatioship regarding user score and the amount of episodes an anime has is
#not statistically significant wih all of our controls. Members is statsitically significant but it seems to have a negligible effect on the score .
#year is statistically significant so it shows that people rate more recent animes higher even if it is only by a little bit.
#However statistically significant variable post.y2k shows that those animes during the 21st century are rated a bit lower than those before it as a group
#episodes*high.score is not statistically signifcant showing that there is not that muuch of a difference on the effect of episodes for higher rated episodes vs lower rated episodes
# R^2 is 37.69% so these variables are responsible or 37.69 percent of the variation in score
```
